text:
don't use card containers -- those are for the web and look bad on iphone -- bleed-edge instead. [...] - Full-bleed, **no card containers** — glass reserved only for floating controls.
- **TikTok-style runner**: one set per full-screen page, tap/swipe to advance. [...] Targets iOS 26 Liquid Glass APIs throughout (`.glassEffect()`, `.glassEffect(.regular.tint(...).interactive(), in:)`, `GlassEffectContainer`, `.buttonStyle(.glass)` / `.glassProminent`), reserved strictly for floating controls per the hard design rules — no card containers anywhere. Pure SwiftUI, no third-party packages, Swift language mode 5 compatible, single `@main`. I did not run xcodebuild/xcodegen per your instructions. [...] the UI should be tik-tok like where I only see what set I need to do next and perhaps cues or something that can be attached by the coach for the exercise. [...] - **TikTok-style runner**: one set per full-screen page, tap/swipe to advance.
- **Circuits & supersets** first-class: block model with rounds + an inline movement mini-map (A1 ▶ A2 …). [...] **The runner** (the hero): TikTok-style, one set per full-screen page, tap **Log & Next** or swipe to advance.
- **Full-bleed, no cards** — background fills the screen edge-to-edge; glass is reserved for the floating top strip and bottom control cluster only. [...] - **Theme.swift** — `MoodKey` → gradient/glow color mapping per movement, and `BackgroundView`, the full-bleed background that `.ignoresSafeArea()` so it bleeds under the Dynamic Island and under the floating bottom bar.
- **Haptics.swift** — thin wrapper over `UIImpactFeedbackGenerator`/`UISelectionFeedbackGenerator`/`UINotificationFeedbackGenerator`, each calling `.prepare()` before firing. [...] - **Circuits & supersets are first-class** — `SUPERSET A · ROUND 1 OF 3` with an inline mini-map (`▶ A1 Incline DB Press · A2 Barbell Row`), block hue shifts per section (chest = crimson, superset = purple), plus rest pages between rounds.
- **Coach cues** attached per exercise, in a quiet glass quote pill.
